author: dashboard-bootstrap
content: Plan (dual-write shape, agreed with user): (1) New module src/hub_v3.rs: RefWriter using git plumbing only -- agent_ref_name() = refs/crosslink/agents/<id>; append_event_to_ref(): read tip via rev-parse (absent = genesis), read events.log blob from tip, append NDJSON line, hash-object -w, mktree, commit-tree -p tip, update-ref WITH old-value CAS (local compare-and-swap); push_agent_ref(): plain non-force refspec push = remote CAS. cfg(test) fault-injection point for crash harness. (2) Config key hub_v3.dual_write (default false) in config_registry following existing key read patterns. (3) Integration at the SINGLE production append site, shared_writer/core.rs emit_compact_push: after append_event, if flag on, mirror envelope to own agent ref + push best-effort -- shadow failures tracing::warn and never fail the user operation. (4) crosslink integrity hubv3: parity check, shadow-ref event stream must be a suffix-subset of the v2 log matched by (agent_id, agent_seq) with byte-identical envelopes; PASS/FAIL verdict for soak monitoring. State-level parity deferred to PR3 when genesis migration seeds full history. (5) Tests: RefWriter unit (append/genesis/CAS-conflict), crash-injection abort-at-each-step (ref is old-or-new, next write succeeds), two-agent same-repo concurrency (zero conflicts), dual-write smoke via SharedWriter, parity-detects-tampering. REQ-8 single-lock unification deferred to PR4 (shadow writes run under the existing hub write lock already held in emit_compact_push). Soak: flag off by default; user opts in locally post-merge; smoke test exercises parity in CI via cargo test.
